cars.name: Solstice GT
cars.description: The studio poster car: stable on sweepers, forgiving in traffic, and fast enough to feel expensive.
cars.flavorText: Chosen by drivers who want the postcard route to still feel heroic when the timer tightens.

cars.name: Monarch XR
cars.description: The fastest car in the line-up. It carries speed brilliantly but asks for cleaner inputs once the road tightens.
cars.flavorText: Best when you trust the elevated line and keep the wheel calm through the late skyline sweepers.

unlockRule.label: Intended early unlock after proving a silver-pace run.

cars.name: Aero Vista
cars.description: Sharper turn-in and easy recovery make this the safest pick if you want to flirt with near misses and late route choices.
cars.flavorText: Feels lighter on its feet, happiest when the run asks for decisions more than raw velocity.

music_packs.name: Sunset Vector
music_packs.description: A golden-hour lead line with enough lift to sell the opening coast road immediately.
music_packs.previewCue: Warm pads, bright lead, postcard optimism.

routeMood.harbor: Keeps the harbor branch playful and expensive.
routeMood.neon: Turns the expressway into a glamorous late-summer sprint.

music_packs.name: Slipstream Serenade
music_packs.description: More rhythmic and playful, tuned for traffic weaving and that first checkpoint rush.
music_packs.previewCue: Percussive bass pulse and a breezy lead figure.

routeMood.harbor: Pairs especially well with the low-road dock rhythm.
routeMood.neon: Adds a little lounge confidence to the skyline rush.

music_packs.name: Night Circuit
music_packs.description: A darker expressway groove for the city branch, with a cooler low end and cleaner attack.
music_packs.previewCue: Cold bassline, glassy lead, late-night momentum.

routeMood.harbor: Makes the docks feel tense and expensive.
routeMood.neon: The intended soundtrack for the elevated neon charge.

unlockRule.label: Intended unlock after a strong first complete run.

stages.label: Azure Coast
stages.description: Sunset cliffs, palms, and warm ocean glare.
stages.destinationPostcardText: Azure Coast opens like a brochure: orange sea light, palms, and a horizon that already promises a city worth chasing.

stages.label: Mirage Causeway
stages.description: Long bridge spans, sodium lamps, and the first skyline hints.
stages.destinationPostcardText: The causeway is the promise shot: a bridge of sodium lamps with Mirador City beginning to glow in the distance.

stages.label: Harbor Slip
stages.description: Dock cranes, warehouse strips, and stacked brake lights.
stages.destinationPostcardText: Harbor Slip feels industrial and alive: cranes cut across the skyline while brake lights stack under the docks.

stages.label: Neon Express
stages.description: Elevated ribbon roads with billboard light and city glow.
stages.destinationPostcardText: Neon Express is the premium fantasy: elevated ribbon roads, cold signage, and a skyline that feels close enough to touch.

stages.label: Marina Underpass
stages.description: Ferry windows, service tunnels, and warm dockside afterglow.
stages.destinationPostcardText: Marina Underpass trades big-crane drama for ferries, service tunnels, and warm reflections under the hillside roads.

stages.label: Crown Vista
stages.description: Terrace ramps, rooftop glass, and a colder hilltop climb.
stages.destinationPostcardText: Crown Vista is the hilltop fantasy: terrace ramps, rooftop glass, and the observatory ring burning through the mist ahead.

stages.label: Skyline Finish
stages.description: The final rush under towers and reflected glass.
stages.destinationPostcardText: Mirador Skyline closes the run with tower reflections, festival light, and just enough traffic to keep the city feeling alive.

stages.label: Afterglow Heights
stages.description: The final ascent through hilltop terraces and observatory light.
stages.destinationPostcardText: Afterglow Heights closes the run with terrace lights, observatory glow, and a final climb that feels more celebratory than hostile.

route_tree.title: Azure Coast Run
route_tree.story: Carry the Mirador Skyline festival master tape from the coast road to the city before the last gate closes.

branches.name: Harbor Slip
branches.strapline: Low road, dock lights, tighter rhythm
branches.description: The scenic lower line. It ducks toward cranes, ferry lights, and denser traffic pockets before the skyline opens up.
branches.risk: Higher weave density, slightly calmer curves.
branches.scenicMomentSubtitle: Low road postcard under cranes and stacked brake lights.

branches.name: Neon Express
branches.strapline: High road, open throttle, city glow
branches.description: The glamorous fast route. Broader sweepers, heavier speed, and a cleaner charge into the financial district.
branches.risk: Faster flow, wider curves, larger collision penalties at speed.
branches.scenicMomentSubtitle: High road postcard with billboards, glass, and blue neon haze.

presentation.titleBody: Azure Coast Run should feel like the expensive postcard version of the city delivery fantasy: warm coast start, clean split, and a glamorous arrival under the Mirador lights.
presentation.routeCardBody: This board sells the original Redline fantasy in one pass: palms and sea glare first, then a choice between dock rhythm and neon altitude before Mirador opens ahead.
presentation.soundscapeTitle: Warm wind, brake-light hiss, skyline bloom
presentation.soundscapeBody: The road mood here is seaside wash into sodium lamps and finally mirrored glass. It should feel like the soundtrack is chasing the horizon with you, not sitting underneath it.
presentation.destinationGlow: Even before the finish, Azure Coast Run should read as a glossy city-night promise with enough warmth to keep the whole sprint romantic rather than severe.

harbor.title: Harbor arrival
harbor.subtitle: You slipped under the cranes, held the dock rhythm, and delivered the tape with the skyline rising above the water.
harbor.postcard: Mirador Harbor Promenade feels intimate and cinematic: ferry lights below, skyline above, festival glow waiting at the edge of the docks.
harbor.arrivalTitle: Festival barges answer the dock line
harbor.arrivalSubtitle: The last gate opens over the harbor road and the skyline stage answers with reflections off the water, crane lights, and a crowd already leaning over the railings.
harbor.festivalCue: The promenade crowd sees the tape van arrive first, then your taillights. The whole low road feels like it paid off at once.
harbor.soundtrackCue: The selected music pack lands warm and close here, like the final bars are echoing between warehouses and the festival deck.

neon.title: Express arrival
neon.subtitle: You took the elevated line, kept the speed honest, and delivered the tape straight into the city lights.
neon.postcard: Mirador Skyline Deck lands like a premium postcard: glass towers, cold neon, and the stage district opening under the expressway.
neon.arrivalTitle: Skyline lights open like a stage reveal
neon.arrivalSubtitle: The express spills you straight into the festival district: mirrored towers, overpass glow, and a clean final sightline to the Mirador deck.
neon.festivalCue: This payoff feels widescreen and expensive. The city does not just receive the tape; it seems to flare on in response.
neon.soundtrackCue: The chosen music pack hits hardest here, where the last melody feels like it is syncing to signs, glass, and the final tower reflections.

route_trees.title: Azure Coast Run
route_trees.story: Carry the Mirador Skyline festival master tape from the coast road to the city before the last gate closes.

branches.strapline: Low road, dock lights, tighter rhythm
branches.description: The scenic lower line. It ducks toward cranes, ferry lights, and denser traffic pockets before the skyline opens up.
branches.risk: Higher weave density, slightly calmer curves.

branches.scenicMomentSubtitle: High road postcard with billboards, glass, and blue neon haze.

route_trees.title: Afterglow Heights Run
route_trees.strapline: Coast transfer to the hilltop afterparty
route_trees.story: The main stage is already breathing. Now the afterparty cassette has to clear the coast, pick a district line, and make Lumen Heights before the last observatory lights fade.

branches.name: Marina Underpass
branches.strapline: Low line, ferries, amber service road
branches.description: The lower afterparty line. It cuts through the marina service lanes, ferry glare, and amber gantries before the climb toward Lumen Heights.
branches.risk: Tighter packs, calmer arcs, and more tunnel-shadow traffic.
branches.scenicMomentSubtitle: Marina postcard under ferry windows, sodium rails, and warm harbor reflections.

branches.name: Crown Vista
branches.strapline: High line, terrace ramps, electric overlook
branches.description: The upper afterparty line. It takes terrace ramps, rooftop edges, and a colder climb into the hilltop district.
branches.risk: Cleaner flow, faster descents, and harsher punishment near the walls.
branches.scenicMomentSubtitle: High road postcard with balcony lights, hillside glass, and the observatory ring glowing ahead.

presentation.titleBody: Afterglow Heights Run should feel more intimate and elevated than the main festival board: terrace ramps, ferry spillover, observatory light, and a city already glowing below the finish.
presentation.routeCardTitle: Hilltop afterglow over the sleeping city
presentation.routeCardBody: This board is about the second reveal. The coast and causeway return, but the payoff climbs into balconies, hill roads, and the sense that the city is now scenery beneath you.
presentation.soundscapeTitle: Ferry wash, terrace echo, observatory shimmer
presentation.soundscapeBody: The soundstage here should feel closer and more nocturnal: tunnel wash on the lower line, terrace air on the upper line, then a softer, more elevated arrival than Mirador's main-stage charge.
presentation.destinationGlow: Afterglow Heights should feel like the city's private encore: fewer crowds in frame, more balcony light, and a finish that lands as a late-night invitation instead of a headline spectacle.

setpieces.speaker: Dispatch
setpieces.title: Ferry spillover ahead
setpieces.subtitle: Service-road traffic is unloading across the marina tunnel. Read the gaps early and keep the cassette away from the barriers.

setpieces.speaker: Rival / Sera
setpieces.title: Sera closes the overlook
setpieces.subtitle: Terrace traffic stacks across the ramp. Split the lane clean, keep the cassette alive, and do not brush the wall.

harbor.title: Marina terrace arrival
harbor.subtitle: You held the lower line through the ferries and amber gantries, then climbed into the afterparty lights with the cassette still warm.
harbor.postcard: Lumen Heights Marina Terrace feels intimate and expensive in a different way: ferry decks below, hillside lights above, and an afterparty that sounds close before you can see it.
harbor.arrivalTitle: The terrace opens above the ferry lane
harbor.arrivalSubtitle: The last climb clears the marina and the afterparty unfolds in warm tiers of balcony light, rail reflections, and a crowd already turned toward the overlook.
harbor.festivalCue: This payoff feels social and close-up. The cassette reaches the terrace and the whole lower route suddenly reads like a deliberate warm-up set.
harbor.soundtrackCue: The chosen music pack lands with a softer afterglow here, as if the final bars are climbing the hillside with you.

neon.title: Observatory arrival
neon.subtitle: You took the upper terraces, kept the pace clean, and delivered the afterparty cassette straight into the observatory glow.
neon.postcard: Lumen Heights Observatory Ring lands as a hilltop postcard: terrace glass, amber beacons, and the city stretched below the overlook.
neon.arrivalTitle: The hilltop opens into an afterparty crown
neon.arrivalSubtitle: The terrace ramps spill into a circular overlook where the observatory lights, rooftop glass, and city afterglow feel like the whole climb was staged for this reveal.
neon.festivalCue: This payoff feels elevated and ceremonial. The city sits below you while the afterparty ring takes the cassette and turns the whole climb into a finish line.
neon.soundtrackCue: The chosen music pack lands crisp and panoramic here, as if the last melody is stretching across the whole skyline at once.

story_beats.speaker: Dispatch
story_beats.title: Festival tape is live cargo
story_beats.subtitle: Mirador Skyline wants the master reel before the stage lights cut. No heroics, just speed.

story_beats.speaker: DJ Vale
story_beats.title: Keep the music alive
story_beats.subtitle: The route picks the mood. Harbor stays intimate. Neon goes wide-screen.

story_beats.speaker: Rival / Sera
story_beats.title: I can see your taillights from the bridge
story_beats.subtitle: Take the high road if you want the glamorous line. Take the docks if you can actually drive.

story_beats.speaker: Dispatch
story_beats.title: Route split ahead
story_beats.subtitle: Harbor Slip keeps the skyline low and busy. Neon Express is cleaner, faster, and less forgiving.

story_beats.speaker: DJ Vale
story_beats.title: Harbor Slip postcard
story_beats.subtitle: Container lights smear across the water while the cranes slice the moonlight above the docks.

story_beats.speaker: Rival / Sera
story_beats.title: You chose the harder rhythm
story_beats.subtitle: Good. The dock road only looks calm until the brake lights stack and the lane disappears.

story_beats.speaker: DJ Vale
story_beats.title: Neon Express postcard
story_beats.subtitle: The elevated line opens and the city turns into ribbons of glass, signs, and impossible blue light.

story_beats.speaker: Rival / Sera
story_beats.title: That skyline lane suits you
story_beats.subtitle: Don't admire it too long. The express is generous right up until it isn't.

story_beats.speaker: Dispatch
story_beats.title: Final approach
story_beats.subtitle: Stage lights are visible from the overpass. Keep the tape clean and the car pointed at Mirador.

story_beats.speaker: Dispatch
story_beats.title: Afterparty cassette is moving
story_beats.subtitle: Main stage is breathing. Lumen Heights wants the afterparty reel before the observatory lights cycle down.

story_beats.speaker: DJ Vale
story_beats.title: Same city, different landing
story_beats.subtitle: Harbor keeps it warm and close. Crown Vista opens the whole hilltop like a reveal shot.

story_beats.speaker: Rival / Sera
story_beats.title: You still owe the city one more run
story_beats.subtitle: Anyone can hit the skyline once. The hilltop asks whether you can land the afterglow too.

story_beats.speaker: Dispatch
story_beats.title: Heights split ahead
story_beats.subtitle: Marina Underpass stays warmer and tighter. Crown Vista climbs cleaner, faster, and closer to the walls.

story_beats.speaker: DJ Vale
story_beats.title: Marina Underpass postcard
story_beats.subtitle: Ferry windows drift above the service road while amber rails pull the whole lower line toward the hilltop.

story_beats.speaker: Rival / Sera
story_beats.title: Warm route, sharp traffic
story_beats.subtitle: The marina line looks forgiving until the tunnel shadows close and the packs stop giving lane.

story_beats.speaker: DJ Vale
story_beats.title: Crown Vista postcard
story_beats.subtitle: Terrace glass catches the whole city at once and the observatory ring starts to read like a finish line in the sky.

story_beats.speaker: Rival / Sera
story_beats.title: High line, no excuses
story_beats.subtitle: You wanted the overlook. Keep it clean, because those terrace walls turn tiny mistakes into a full reset.

story_beats.speaker: Dispatch
story_beats.title: Observatory final
story_beats.subtitle: Hilltop lights are live. Keep the cassette steady and let the afterparty reveal land on time.
